title: Arrays & Lists
description: Work with collections of data using arrays and lists.
fullDescription: Learn to work with collections of data using arrays and lists. Master iteration and manipulation of multiple data items.

teacherInstructions.setup: Prepare index cards labeled 0 to 9 and object cards to physically model list positions.
steps: Introduce arrays as ordered collections with index positions (5 min).
Demonstrate reading and updating list items by index (7 min).
Students build a list-based mini tracker (10 min).
Practice looping through arrays to summarize results (8 min).
discussionPrompts: Why do arrays use positions starting at zero in many tools?
When would a list be better than many separate variables?
How can loops and arrays work together efficiently?
tips: Use visual index mapping before moving to code.
Check boundary cases like first and last index explicitly.
Have students narrate list changes after each operation.

teacherGuide: Explain arrays as collections of similar items. Show how indexing works. Demonstrate iterating through arrays with loops. Practice common array operations like adding, removing, and searching. Relate arrays to real-world concepts like shopping lists.

studentExercises.title: Create an Array
studentExercises.description: Create an array and access its elements

studentExercises.title: Iterate Arrays
studentExercises.description: Loop through an array and perform operations

studentExercises.title: Array Manipulation
studentExercises.description: Create a program that adds, removes, and searches in arrays
